title: yakRNA Design: A semantic multimodal RNA composer
abstract: Like proteins, RNAs have been a target for exploitation to generate synthetic molecules that can adopt enhanced and novel functions. High-throughput assays, combined with meticulous biochemistry, have led to the generation of some artificial RNAs but the ability to algorithmically program RNAs with an intended function remains difficult. While generative models have revolutionized protein design, RNA design remains challenging due to the dearth of 3D RNA structures. Here we present You Always Know RNA (yakRNA) Design, a frontier language model that can simultaneously reason over sequence, biological function, consensus sequence, and secondary structure to generate functional RNAs. yakRNA Design is not trained on any 3D structural information but rather co-learned over a corpus of semantically labeled sequences that we designed. yakRNA Design, without any human intervention, fine-tuning, sequence optimization, scoring or selection, zero-shot designed 17 (out of 84 total designs) synthetic RNAs that were able to efficiently induce ribosomes to change their reading frames during elongation at rates comparable to or higher than most natural sequences. One of these efficient synthetic RNAs was found to have no identity to any sequence in the known universe, demonstrating how a model with semantic understanding of RNA has rich generative capabilities.

title: Aiki-XP: leakage-controlled multimodal prediction of within-species relative protein expression at pan-bacterial scale
abstract: Generalizable protein-expression prediction can accelerate protein engineering, inform disease mechanisms, and help optimize heterologous recombinant protein production. Protein expression is governed by many interacting parameters that no single omics view captures. We develop Aiki-XP, a multimodal platform integrating four biological scales (genome, operon, coding sequence, protein) plus biophysical features across 492,026 genes from 385 bacterial species. Aiki-XP predicts within-species relative abundance (per-species z-score rank), not absolute copies per cell. Under a leakage-controlled gene-operon split Aiki-XP reaches Spearman {rho}nc = 0.592 versus 0.509 for ESM-C 600M alone, and each tier of a monotone protein[->]operon[->]genome deployment ladder yields a statistically significant gain; a five-recipe rank-average ensemble adds a further +0.016. All recipes were locked before external evaluation; transfer to heterologous, cross-species, and novel-phylum benchmarks demonstrates utility and limits. Ablations and scaling experiments identify operon-scale genomic context, not protein language-model capacity, as the rate-limiting input at this scale; one foundation model per biological scale suffices, with same-scale stacking adding little.

title: Autonomous multimodal agents enable transparent, spatiotemporal reconstruction of immune dynamics in pancreatic cancer progression
abstract: Pancreatic cancer progression is orchestrated by dynamic shifts in immune and stromal cellular ecosystems, yet the temporal and spatial principles governing these transitions remain poorly understood. Here, we present an agentic computational pathology framework that leverages large language models to orchestrate modular biomarker inference and spatiotemporal reasoning directly from routine H&E histology. Our approach, ROSIE (RObust in Silico Immunofluorescence), combines deep-learning-based multiplex inference with LLM-driven agent logic that emulates pathologist-level reasoning, enabling transparent and reproducible analysis of complex tissue microarchitectures. Applying this workflow to pancreatic intraepithelial neoplasia (PanIN) progression in KSC transgenic mice (n=24, ages 4-12 weeks), we generated 10.44 million single-cell profiles and identified a temporally ordered immune trajectory comprising three spatially distinct immune-stromal states: (1) early immune-surveillance niche: sharply bounded window of adaptive immune activation and antigen-presentation enrichment; (2) transitional mixed state: declining lymphoid activity, emerging exhaustion programs, and early EMT/angiogenesis signals; (3) stromal-dominant terminal state: fibroblast expansion, vascular remodeling, and immune silence. These findings establish pancreatic cancer progression as a temporally ordered sequence of immune activation, exhaustion, and stromal takeover. The agentic framework transcends static AI models by offering dynamic, tool-augmented reasoning that bridges high-dimensional tissue data with clinical interpretability -- providing a scalable foundation for identifying therapeutic inflection points in early tumor evolution.

title: Multimodal Protein Language Models for Enzyme Kinetic Parameters: From Substrate Recognition to Conformational Adaptation
abstract: Predicting enzyme kinetic parameters quantifies how efficiently an enzyme catalyzes a specific substrate under defined biochemical conditions. Canonical parameters such as the turnover number ($k_\text{cat}$), Michaelis constant ($K_\text{m}$), and inhibition constant ($K_\text{i}$) depend jointly on the enzyme sequence, the substrate chemistry, and the conformational adaptation of the active site during binding. Many learning pipelines simplify this process to a static compatibility problem between the enzyme and substrate, fusing their representations through shallow operations and regressing a single value. Such formulations overlook the staged nature of catalysis, which involves both substrate recognition and conformational adaptation. In this regard, we reformulate kinetic prediction as a staged multimodal conditional modeling problem and introduce the Enzyme-Reaction Bridging Adapter (ERBA), which injects cross-modal information via fine-tuning into Protein Language Models (PLMs) while preserving their biochemical priors. ERBA performs conditioning in two stages: Molecular Recognition Cross-Attention (MRCA) first injects substrate information into the enzyme representation to capture specificity; Geometry-aware Mixture-of-Experts (G-MoE) then integrates active-site structure and routes samples to pocket-specialized experts to reflect induced fit. To maintain semantic fidelity, Enzyme-Substrate Distribution Alignment (ESDA) enforces distributional consistency within the PLM manifold in a reproducing kernel Hilbert space. Experiments across three kinetic endpoints and multiple PLM backbones, ERBA delivers consistent gains and stronger out-of-distribution performance compared with sequence-only and shallow-fusion baselines, offering a biologically grounded route to scalable kinetic prediction and a foundation for adding cofactors, mutations, and time-resolved structural cues.

title: Dissecting clinical reasoning failures in frontier artificial intelligence using 10,000 synthetic cases
abstract: Background: Current medical large language model (LLM) evaluations largely rely on small collections of cases, whereas rigorous safety testing requires large-scale, diverse, and complex cases with verifiable ground truth. Multiple Sclerosis (MS) provides an ideal evaluation model, with validated diagnostic criteria and numerous paraclinical tests informing differential diagnosis, investigation, and management. Methods: We generated synthetic MS cases with ground-truth labels for diagnosis, localisation, and management. Four frontier LLMs (Gemini 3 Pro/Flash, GPT 5.2/5 mini) were instructed to analyse cases to provide anatomical localisation, differential diagnoses, investigations, and management plans. An automated evaluator compared these outputs to the ground-truth labels. Blinded subspecialty experts validated 70 cases for realism and automated evaluator accuracy. We then evaluated LLM decision-making across 1,000 cases and scaled to 10,000 to characterise rare, catastrophic failures. Results: Subspecialist expert review confirmed 100% synthetic case realism and 99.8% (95% CI 95.5 to 100) automated evaluation accuracy. Across 1,000 generated MS cases, all LLMs successfully included MS in the differential diagnoses for more than 91% cases. However, diagnostic competence did not associate with treatment safety. Gemini 3 models had low rates of clinically appropriate steroid recommendations (Flash: 7.2% 95% CI 5.6 to 8.8; Pro: 15.8% 95% CI 13.6 to 18.1) compared to GPT 5 mini (23.5% 95% CI 20.8 to 26.1), frequently overlooking contraindications like active infection. OpenAI models inappropriately recommended acute intravenous thrombolysis for MS cases (9.6% GPT 5.2; 6.4% GPT 5 mini) compared to below 1% for Gemini models. Expanded evaluation (to 10,000 cases) probed these errors in detail. Thrombolysis was recommended in 10.1% of cases lacking symptom timing information and paradoxically persisted (2.9%) even when symptoms were explicitly documented as more than 14 days old. Conclusion: Automated expert-level evaluation across 10,000 cases characterised artificial intelligence clinical blind spots hitherto invisible to small-scale testing. Massive-scale simulation and automated interrogation should become standard for uncovering serious failures and implementing safety guardrails before clinical deployment exposes patients to risk.

title: Large language models and retrieval augmented generation for complex clinical codelists: evaluating performance and assessing failure modes
abstract: Objectives: Large language models (LLMs) have shown promise in creating clinical codelists for research purposes, a time-consuming task requiring expert domain knowledge. Here, we evaluate the performance and assess failure modes of a retrieval augmented generation (RAG) approach to creating clinical codelists for the large and complex medical terminology used by the Clinical Practice Research Datalink (CPRD). Materials & Methods: We set up a RAG system using a database of word embeddings of the medical terminology that we created using a general-purpose word embedding model (gemini-embedding). We developed 7 reference codelists presenting different challenges and tagged required and optional codes. We ran 168 evaluations (7 codelists, 2 different database subsets, 4 models, 3 epochs each). Scoring was based on the omission of required codes, and inclusion of irrelevant codes. We used model-grading (i.e., grading by another LLM with the reference codelists provided as context) to evaluate the output codelists (a score of 0% being all incorrect and 100% being all correct). Results: We saw varying accuracy across models and codelists, with Gemini 3 Pro (Score 43%) generally performing better than Claude Sonnet 4.6 (36%), Gemini 3 Flash, and OpenAI GPT 5.2 performing worst (14%). Models performed better with shorter target codelists (e.g., Eosinophilic esophagitis with four codes, and Hidradenitis suppurativa with 14 codes). For example, all models consistently failed to produce a complete Wrist fracture codelist (with 214 required codes). We further present evaluation summaries, and failure mode evaluations produced by parsing LLM chat logs. Discussion: Besides demonstrating that a single-shot RAG approach is currently not suitable for codelist generation, we demonstrate failure modes including hallucinations, retrieval failures and generation failures where retrieved codes are not used. Conclusions: Our findings suggest that while RAG systems using current frontier LLMs may create correct clinical codelists in some cases, they still struggle with large and complex terminologies and codelists with a large number of codes. The failure mode we highlight can inform the creation of future workflows to avoid failures.

title: MIMIC-IV-Phenotype-Atlas (MIPA) : A Publicly Available Dataset for EHR Phenotyping
abstract: Introduction Secondary use of electronic health records (EHRs) often requires transforming raw clinical information into research-grade data. A central step in this process is EHR phenotyping - the identification of patient cohorts defined by specific medical conditions. Although numerous approaches exist, from ICD-based heuristics to supervised learning and large language models (LLMs), the field lacks standardized benchmark datasets, limiting reproducibility and hindering fair comparison across methods. Methods We developed the MIMIC-IV Phenotype Atlas (MIPA) dataset, an adaptation of MIMIC-IV that provides expert-annotated discharge summaries across 16 phenotypes of varying prevalence and complexity. Two independent clinicians reviewed and labeled the discharge summaries, resolving disagreements by consensus. In parallel, we implemented a processing pipeline that extracts multimodal EHR features and generates training, validation, and testing datasets for supervised phenotyping. To illustrate MIPA's utility, we benchmarked four phenotyping methods : ICD-based classifiers, keyword-driven Term Frequency-Inverse Document Frequency (TF-IDF) classifiers, supervised machine learning (ML) models, and LLMs on the task. Results The final MIPA corpus consists of 1,388 expert-annotated discharge summaries. Annotation reliability was high (mean document-level kappa = 0.805, mean label-level kappa = 0.771), with 91% of disagreements resolved through consensus review. MIPA provides high-quality phenotype labels paired with structured EHR features and predefined train/validation/test splits for each phenotype. In the benchmarking case study, LLMs achieved the highest F1 scores in 13 of 16 phenotypes, particularly for conditions requiring contextual interpretation of clinical narrative, while supervised ML offered moderate improvements over rule-based baselines. Conclusion MIPA is the first publicly available benchmark dataset dedicated to EHR phenotyping, combining expert-curated annotations, broad phenotype coverage, and a reproducible processing pipeline. By enabling standardized comparison across ICD-based heuristics, ML models, and LLMs, MIPA provides a durable reference resource to advance methodological development in automated phenotyping.

title: RVQ-Alpha: Bridging Single-Cell Transcriptomics and Large Language Models via Discrete Tokenization and Verifiable Reinforcement Learning
abstract: Integrating continuous single-cell gene expression profiles with discrete-token large language models (LLMs) remains an open challenge: text-based methods are token-inefficient and discard quantitative precision, continuous embeddings preclude autoregressive generation, and single-codebook vector quantization cannot stratify biological scales. We present RVQ-Alpha, an end-to-end framework that bridges this modality gap through three contributions: (i) a Residual Vector Quantization (RVQ) tokenizer that compresses each cell into a fixed 10-token sequence via eight residual codebooks embedded directly in the LLM vocabulary, requiring 3.4x fewer tokens than prior discrete methods and enabling bidirectional unification of cell interpretation and generation; (ii) scCoT-Synth, a teacher-student engine that grounds newly added biological tokens through evidence-before-conclusion reasoning, using the language modeling objective as the cross-modal alignment signal without a separate projection network; and (iii) a Fact-Aware RLVR system combining an ontology-grounded answer judge with saliency-weighted verification of biological claims against actual expression data, under dynamic gating that conditions hallucination suppression on task competence. Built on Qwen3-4B and trained via continued pretraining, supervised fine-tuning, and reinforcement learning, RVQ-Alpha substantially improves out-of-distribution generalization and rare-cell recognition across eight held-out datasets; ablations confirm that evidence-first grounding reduces hallucination more than fivefold.

title: Comparing prognostic performance and reasoning between large language models and physicians
abstract: Importance: Physicians routinely prognosticate to guide care delivery and shared decision making, particularly when caring for patients with critical illnesses. Yet, these physician estimates are prone to inaccuracy and uncertainty. Artificial intelligence, including large language models (LLMs), show promise in supporting or improving this prognostication. However, the performance of contemporary LLMs in prognosticating for the heterogeneous population of critically ill patients remains poorly understood. Objective: To characterize and compare the performance of LLMs and physicians when predicting 6-month mortality for hospitalized adults who survived critical illness. Design: Embedded mixed methods study with elicitation and comparison of prognostic estimates and reasoning from LLMs and practicing physicians. Setting: The publicly available, deidentified Medical Information Mart for Intensive Care (MIMIC)-IV v2.2 dataset. Participants: We randomly selected 100 hospitalizations of adult survivors of critical illness. Four contemporary LLMs (Open AI GPT-4o, o3- and o4-mini, and DeepSeek-R1) and 7 physicians provided independent prognostic estimates for each case (1,100 total estimates; 400 LLM and 700 physician). Main outcomes and measures: For each case, LLMs and physicians used the hospital discharge summary and demographics to predict 6-month mortality (yes/no) and provide their reasoning (free text). We assessed prognostic performance using accuracy, sensitivity, and specificity, and used inductive, qualitative content analysis to characterize reasonings. Results: Mean physician accuracy for predicting mortality was 70.1% (95% CI 63.7-76.4%), with sensitivity of 59.7% (95% CI 50.6-68.8%) and specificity of 80.6% (95% CI 71.7-88.2%). The top-performing LLM (OpenAI o4-mini) accuracy was 78.0% (95% CI 70.0-86.0%), with sensitivity of 80.0% (95% CI 67.4-90.2%) and specificity of 76.0% (95% CI 63.3-88.0%). The difference between mean physician and top-performing LLM accuracy was not statistically significant (p = 0.5). Qualitative analysis revealed similar patterns in LLM and physician expressed reasoning, except that physicians regularly and explicitly reported uncertainty while LLMs did not. Conclusion and Relevance: In this study, LLMs and physicians achieved comparable, moderate performance in predicting 6-month mortality after critical illness, with similar patterns in expressed reasoning. Our findings suggest LLMs could be used to support prognostication in clinical practice but also raise safety concerns due to the lack of LLM uncertainty expression.

title: Large Language Model Agent Enables Autonomous Machine Learning Model Building for Biomedicine
abstract: Machine learning accelerates biomedical discovery, but creating effective predictive models requires specialized human expertise and demanding manual effort. Researchers must iteratively design pipelines, select architectures, and debug code. This challenge is particularly severe in biomedicine because of the heterogeneous datasets, sparse annotations, and complex evaluation protocols one must deal with. We present AIDO.Harness, an agentic artificial intelligence system that fully automates the entire life-cycle of biomedical model development. Provided only with a natural language task description and a target metric, AIDO.Harness autonomously constructs executable training and evaluation pipelines. The system selects optimal modeling strategies, executes experiments, and uses automated feedback-loop to iteratively revise its own code, configurations, and training procedures. It flexibly adapts to new tasks by training specialized models de novo or refining pretrained foundation models. We show that across diverse biomedical benchmarks, AIDO.Harness produces highly competitive solutions against human alternatives, while eliminating the manual iteration previously required for robust model development. By automating the translation of raw data into reliable AI models, AIDO.Harness demonstrates how AI itself can be used to accelerate AI for biomedical research.

title: Back to BERT in 2026: ModernGENA as a Strong, Efficient Baseline for DNA Foundation Models
abstract: Recent advances in DNA language models have mainly come from building larger and more complex architectures, making it harder to understand the effect of changes to standard components such as the transformer layers widely used in NLP. In this work, we study whether and how a modernized BERT-style backbone (ModernBERT) can be adapted to genomic sequence modeling to improve computational efficiency, training stability, and downstream performance. Under controlled experimental settings, we benchmark efficiency across a range of sequence lengths and evaluate downstream performance on the Nucleotide Transformer benchmark. The resulting model, ModernGENA, achieves a strong efficiency-quality trade-off and ranks among the top-performing models in our evaluation suite. To support reproducibility and provide a solid default reference point for future architectural work in genomics, we release the full implementation and configuration of ModernGENA as an open, reusable baseline, and make ModernGENA base and ModernGENA large publicly available through the DNA language models collection on Hugging Face.

title: Cross-Attention Over RNA And Protein Sequences Enables Generalizable Interaction Prediction
abstract: Computational predictions are essential to characterize the RNA-protein interaction landscape, yet a persistent gap between benchmark performance and practical utility suggests that current models have limited generalization capabilities. To address this issue, we present CORAL (Cross-attention for RNA-protein Association Learning), a deep learning framework for the prediction of RNA--protein interactions that integrates pretrained protein (ESM-2) and RNA (DNABERT2) language models through bidirectional cross-attention with Low-Rank Adaptation fine-tuning. We also introduce a benchmarking framework that rigorously addresses the problem of data redundancy between training and test sets, which greatly inflates model performances reported in the literature. To this end we adopt three partitioning strategies of increasing stringency: conventional random splits, pairwise non-redundant splits, and component-wise non-redundant splits. CORAL maintains an F1 score of 0.65 under the most stringent component-wise evaluation, compared to 0.55 for the next-best method. Interpretability analyses reveal that specific cross-attention heads systematically attend to structurally defined contact positions between RNA and protein molecules, showing 27% elevated attention at interface residues across 309 experimentally resolved complexes (p < 0.01). These findings establish that current RPI prediction benchmarks substantially inflate performance estimates and demonstrate that cross-modal attention architectures yield improved generalization alongside mechanistically interpretable representations.

title: A Scalable Method for Validated Data Extraction from Electronic Health Records with Large Language Models
abstract: Purpose/Background: Healthcare organizations increasingly require structured, patient-level clinical variables for treatment decisions, operational workflows, quality measurement, and clinical trial screening. Relevant information is often fragmented across heterogeneous EHR systems, unstructured formats, and scanned documents. Large language models (LLMs) offer an opportunity to enhance medical record data extraction, particularly in oncology where point-of-care structured coding is often insufficient to capture a patient's longitudinal clinical course. Methods: Two complementary LLM-based approaches were developed. In the first, LLMs performed schema-based named entity recognition and relation extraction from unstructured documents; extracted variables were normalized to FHIR and OHDSI vocabularies. In the second, a retrieval-augmented checklist framework queried and integrated embedded document text with extracted structured data using prescriptive prompts to answer specific clinical questions, returning custom outputs with evidence-based justifications and source-document citations. Performance was evaluated through human validation, automated consistency checks, and iterative error analysis. Results: Schema-based extractors designed to extract medications, radiation, and surgical procedure fields achieved ~95% accuracy, precision, recall, and F1. Deployed across 3,493 patients, LLM extraction yielded 71% more total medication records and a 207% increase in distinct oncology drug ingredients over structured C-CDA sources. Oncology therapies were captured for 65% of patients versus 40% in structured data, with dramatically improved clinical attribute coverage: indication for prescription (70.5% vs. 4.6%) and reason for discontinuation (9.7% vs. 0%). For radiation and surgical procedures - largely absent from structured records - LLM extraction yielded a 715% and 422% increase, respectively. The checklist extraction framework achieved significant F1 scores when designed to extract cancer diagnosis variables (99.0%) and lines of therapy (97.6%) across 4,802 validated elements. Cancer diagnoses and dates were identified for 93.5% of patients versus 69.8% in structured data; stage and grade were extracted for 64% and 62% of patients versus near-zero structured availability. A lines-of-therapy checklist generated 4,218 treatment lines across 2,320 patients, capturing regimen dates, best response, discontinuation reasons, and progression dates. Among patients with response data, objective response rate declined from 64.7% (95% CI 59.0-70.0%) at first line to 21.5% (95% CI 13.3-33.0%) at third line, and first-line response rates varied across tumor types from 42.9% in colorectal to 85.0% in esophageal cancer. Conclusions: Leveraging two complementary LLM-based strategies, we substantially enhanced the completeness and utility of structured clinical data from heterogeneous medical records, supporting scalable generation of interoperable patient-level datasets for clinical analytics, research, and operational workflows.

title: Unsupervised protein language models learn patterns of enzyme function
abstract: While enormous amounts of sequence information have become available, assignment of sequence to a particular enzymatic function has remained elusive. Here we describe a framework that drives a general protein language model to find a target reaction without specific training, using an initial bridgehead protein. At the heart of this framework is PLM-clust, an algorithm that employs k-means on top of protein language model embeddings to convert sequence space into functional reservoirs of latent space, and samples from these clusters based on accelerated zero-shot scoring. We demonstrate PLM-clust in a recursive discovery process (with enzyme hit rates quickly rising to >90%), segmenting isofunctional reservoirs and exploring them in greater detail. This approach was exemplified for glycosyl hydrolases (a xylanase, >100-fold activity increase) and for imine reductases (IREDs, >100-fold increase in catalytic promiscuity profiles). PLM-clust reliably brings about novel enzymes that are proficient at the catalytic task at hand, reaching deeply into sequence space with a majority of residues exchanged.
abstract_zh: 尽管目前已有海量的蛋白质序列信息，但将序列准确分配至特定酶功能仍是一项挑战。本研究提出了一种利用通用蛋白质语言模型（PLM）在无需特定训练的情况下寻找目标反应的框架，该框架仅需一个初始“桥头堡”蛋白。其核心算法 PLM-clust 在 PLM 嵌入表示的基础上采用 k-means 聚类，将序列空间转化为潜在空间的功能库，并基于加速的零样本评分从这些簇中进行采样。通过递归发现过程，酶的命中率可迅速提升至 90% 以上。该方法在糖苷水解酶（木聚糖酶活性提高 >100 倍）和亚胺还原酶（IREDs，催化杂泛性谱图提高 >100 倍）中得到了验证。PLM-clust 能够可靠地挖掘出具有高效催化能力的新型酶，且能深入探索序列空间，实现大部分残基被替换的远源序列识别。
why_it_matters: 这篇工作更像方法创新，可能直接关联 metagenomics、long-read 或 pathogen identification 流程优化。
analysis_zh:
## 核心内容
该研究开发了名为 PLM-clust 的无监督框架，利用预训练蛋白质语言模型（PLM）的嵌入向量，通过 k-means 聚类和零样本评分（zero-shot scoring）技术，从海量无标注序列中精准识别具有特定功能的酶。该方法不依赖于特定任务的监督训练，仅需一个已知功能的“桥头堡”蛋白作为起点。

## 为什么相关
在病原微生物研究中，快速鉴定宏基因组数据中的未知功能基因（如新型耐药酶、毒力因子相关酶）至关重要。该工作展示了如何利用基础模型（Foundation Model）在缺乏标注数据的情况下，通过零样本学习实现高准确度的功能注释，直接关联到宏基因组分析和病原体功能基因组学的流程优化。

## 新颖度判断
新颖度较高。不同于传统的基于同源性比对（如 BLAST）或有监督的深度学习分类器，PLM-clust 强调“无监督”和“递归发现”。它通过将连续的潜在空间分割为“功能库”（functional reservoirs），实现了在极低序列同源性（大部分残基已替换）的情况下依然能捕捉到功能模式的能力。

## 方法与证据
- **方法**：采用 PLM-clust 算法，在 PLM 嵌入层上运行 k-means 聚类，并结合加速零样本评分进行递归采样。
- **数据与指标**：实验证明酶命中率可快速升至 90% 以上。在木聚糖酶实验中，获得了活性提升超过 100 倍的变体；在亚胺还原酶（IREDs）实验中，催化杂泛性（promiscuity）提升了 100 倍以上。
- **结论**：该模型能有效识别序列差异巨大但功能保守的酶，突破了传统序列比对的局限。

## 应用价值
1. **功能基因组学**：可用于从临床或环境样本的宏基因组数据中挖掘新型功能蛋白。
2. **酶工程与合成生物学**：通过识别高性能的远源同源物，加速工业酶（如糖苷水解酶）的改造。
3. **病原体研究**：辅助识别病原体中进化压力下序列高度变异但功能关键的代谢酶。

## 局限与风险
- **依赖初始输入**：算法高度依赖于初始“桥头堡蛋白”的选择，摘要未说明若初始蛋白选择不当对结果的影响。
- **计算成本**：摘要未提及大规模 k-means 聚类和 PLM 嵌入提取的计算资源消耗。
- **功能范围**：目前仅在酶类（水解酶、还原酶）上验证，对于非酶蛋白（如结构蛋白或配体结合蛋白）的适用性摘要未说明。

title: Brain2VLM: Hierarchical Alignment Between Cortical Representations and Vision-Language Latent Spaces
abstract: This work introduces Brain2VLM, a framework for analyzing how cortical representations align with latent spaces of pretrained diffusion-based vision-language models for brain-to-image reconstruction. While recent approaches achieve strong performance by mapping functional Magnetic Resonance Imaging (fMRI) signals to model latents, the structure of this mapping remains poorly understood. We hypothesize that brain-to-latent alignment is hierarchical, with early visual cortex exhibiting approximately linear correspondence to structural diffusion latents, and higher-order visual areas requiring nonlinear mappings to align with semantic embedding spaces. To test this, we decode diffusion latents and CLIP embeddings from fMRI signals using both linear ridge regression and a nonlinear residual MLP on the Natural Scenes Dataset. Our results reveal that nonlinear decoding provides only marginal improvements for diffusion latents ({Delta} {approx} 0.05 - 0.06 in correlation), but yields substantial gains for semantic embeddings ({Delta} {approx} 0.47), significantly improving distributional alignment (MMD: 0.042 vs 0.358). However, increased decoder expressivity can introduce shifts in latent distributions, highlighting a trade-off between prediction accuracy and generative compatibility. Despite using a simple reconstruction pipeline, Brain2VLM achieves strong performance (PixCorr: 0.33, CLIP: 85%), suggesting that improvements in brain-to-latent alignment play an important role in reconstruction quality alongside generative modeling. These findings provide empirical evidence for hierarchical alignment between cortical representations and model latent spaces, positioning the brain-to-latent interface as a primary bottleneck in brain decoding systems. Our code can be found at https://github.com/adarsh-crafts/Brain2VLM
abstract_zh: 本研究提出 Brain2VLM 框架，旨在分析皮层表征与预训练扩散视觉语言模型潜空间在脑图重建中的对齐关系。研究假设这种对齐具有层次性：初级视觉皮层与结构化扩散潜空间呈近似线性对应，而高阶视觉区域则需非线性映射以对齐语义嵌入空间。基于 Natural Scenes Dataset (NSD)，研究利用线性岭回归和非线性残差 MLP 从 fMRI 信号解码扩散潜变量和 CLIP 嵌入。结果显示，非线性解码对扩散潜变量提升微小（相关度提升 0.05-0.06），但对语义嵌入提升显著（相关度提升约 0.47），并显著改善了分布对齐（MMD 从 0.358 降至 0.042）。尽管重建流程简单，Brain2VLM 仍实现了 0.33 的 PixCorr 和 85% 的 CLIP 评分。研究证明了皮层表征与模型潜空间的层次化对齐，指出脑-潜空间接口是解码系统的主要瓶颈。
why_it_matters: 这篇工作更像方法创新，可能直接关联 metagenomics、long-read 或 pathogen identification 流程优化。
analysis_zh:
## 核心内容
该研究引入了 Brain2VLM 框架，核心在于探究人类大脑皮层活动（fMRI）与多模态大模型（扩散模型和 CLIP）潜空间之间的对齐机制。研究重点验证了“层次化对齐”假设，即大脑不同区域对视觉信息的处理方式（结构性 vs 语义性）与 AI 模型不同层级的潜空间存在对应关系。

## 为什么相关
本研究属于 AI 与神经科学的强交叉领域。虽然元数据提示可能关联宏基因组或病原鉴定，但从摘要内容看，其核心价值在于“生物信号与多模态大模型（Foundation Models）的对齐”。这种将复杂的生物序列/信号（如 fMRI）映射到预训练模型潜空间的方法论，对于病原体功能基因组学中如何将蛋白质结构/序列映射到语义空间具有潜在的借鉴意义。

## 新颖度判断
新颖度较高（5.75）。不同于以往只追求重建精度的研究，Brain2VLM 首次系统性地量化了线性与非线性解码在不同潜空间（结构化扩散潜变量 vs 语义 CLIP 嵌入）中的贡献差异，并明确提出了“脑-潜空间接口”是当前解码系统的主要瓶颈。

## 方法与证据
- **实验对象与数据**：使用 Natural Scenes Dataset (NSD) 的 fMRI 数据。
- **模型方法**：对比了线性岭回归（Linear Ridge Regression）与非线性残差 MLP（Residual MLP）两种解码器。
- **关键证据**：对于语义嵌入，非线性解码器的相关度提升高达 0.47，最大均值差异（MMD）从 0.358 显著优化至 0.042；而对于扩散潜变量，提升仅为 0.05-0.06。这证明了高阶视觉语义必须通过非线性映射才能与模型对齐。
- **性能指标**：最终实现 PixCorr 0.33 和 CLIP 评分 85%。

## 应用价值
- **脑机接口优化**：通过识别层次化对齐特征，可以针对性地设计解码器，提高从脑电/脑磁信号重建视觉信息的准确度。
- **模型解释性**：为理解视觉语言模型（VLM）的潜空间如何模拟人类生物视觉系统提供了实证依据。
- **通用对齐框架**：其处理生物信号与潜空间分布偏移（Latent Distribution Shift）的思路可推广至其他生物信息学对齐任务。

## 局限与风险
- **分布偏移风险**：摘要指出增加解码器的表达能力（Expressivity）可能会引入潜空间分布的偏移，导致生成兼容性下降。
- **管线简化**：研究使用的是“简单的重建管线”（Simple reconstruction pipeline），可能限制了在更复杂场景下的表现。
- **计算成本**：摘要未说明非线性残差 MLP 在大规模数据集上的训练成本及对不同个体 fMRI 差异的泛化能力。

title: Recovering Clinical Detail in AI-Generated Responses for Low Back Pain Through Prompt Design
abstract: Introduction Large language models are increasingly being used in healthcare. In interventional pain medicine, clinical reasoning is essential for procedural planning. Prior studies show that simplified prompts reduce clinical detail in AI-generated responses. It remains unclear whether this reflects knowledge loss or simply prompt-driven suppression of information. Methods We performed a controlled comparative study using 15 standardized low back pain questions representing common interventional pain questions. Each question was submitted to ChatGPT under three conditions, professional-level prompt (DP), fourth-grade reading-level prompt (D4), and clinician-directed rewriting of the D4 response to a medical level (U4[->]MD). No follow-up prompting was allowed. Three physicians independently rated responses for accuracy using a 0-2 ordinal scale. Clinical completeness was determined by consensus. Word count and Flesch-Kincaid Grade Level (FKGL) were also measured. Paired t-tests compared conditions. Results Accuracy was highest with professional prompting (1.76). Accuracy declined with the fourth-grade prompt (1.33; p = 0.00086). When simplified responses were rewritten for clinicians, accuracy returned to baseline (1.76; p {approx} 1.00 vs DP). Clinical completeness followed the same pattern showing DP 80.0%, D4 6.7%, U4[->]MD 73.3%. Fourth-grade responses were shorter and less complex. Upscaled responses were more complex and similar in length to professional responses. Inter-rater reliability was low (Fleiss {kappa} = 0.17), but trends were consistent across conditions. Conclusions Reduced clinical detail under simplified prompts appears to reflect constrained output rather than loss of knowledge. Clinician-directed reframing restores omitted content. LLM performance in interventional pain depends strongly on prompt design and intended audience.
why_it_matters: 这篇工作偏基础模型/Agent方向，可能影响病原检测任务的建模上限，值得关注其任务定义与评测设计。

title: Drug or Pokemon? An analysis of the ability of large language models to discern fabricated medications
abstract: Background Large language models (LLMs) are increasingly used in medication-related tasks despite limited evidence supporting their accuracy and safety. LLMs are vulnerable to adversarial attacks and may confabulate in response to fabricated information in the prompt. Given the substantial risk of harm from medication errors, the purpose of this study was to establish a benchmark task to evaluate LLM performance in distinguishing fabricated from real medications. Methods Two datasets (brand and generic), each consisting of 250 medication lists were developed, each including 4-6 medications and one fabricated medication (specifically a Pokemon character), with complete dosing information provided. Six LLMs were evaluated (GPT-5-Chat, GPT-4o-mini, DrugGPT, Gemma-3-27B-IT, Llama-3.3-70B-Instruct, and Qwen3-32B). For each medication list, models were queried for dosing information and disease indication across three experimental conditions: standard decoding, standard decoding with mitigation prompting, and deterministic decoding (temperature = 0). Each experiment was performed in triplicate. Outputs were deemed confabulations if the LLM failed to recognize and reject the fabricated medication. The primary outcome was the rate of confabulations. Exact paired-permutation tests were used to compare confabulation rates across LLMs and prompting approaches. Results Confabulation rates under standard conditions ranged from 2.7% to 99.6%. DrugGPT demonstrated the lowest baseline confabulation rates (2.7-6.4%), whereas other models demonstrated substantially higher rates (47.2-99.6%). Mitigation prompting significantly reduced confabulation rates compared to standard and deterministic decoding across all tasks and datasets (p < 0.001): GPT-5-Chat achieved confabulation rates of 0% with mitigation prompting from baseline rates of 66.4-88.5%. Deterministic decoding did not substantially impact confabulation rates. Conclusions LLMs performing medication-related tasks are susceptible to confabulation in response to invalid or fabricated inputs, raising important safety concerns for clinical use. While mitigation prompting reduced confabulation rates, this study underscores the importance of robust safeguards when deploying LLMs in medication-related decision-making.
abstract_zh: 尽管大语言模型（LLM）越来越多地用于药物相关任务，但其准确性和安全性证据有限。本研究旨在建立基准任务，评估 LLM 区分虚构药物与真实药物的能力。研究开发了品牌药和通用药两个数据集，各包含 250 个药物列表，每个列表含 4-6 种真实药物及一个虚构药物（宝可梦角色），并提供完整剂量信息。评估了 GPT-5-Chat、GPT-4o-mini、DrugGPT、Gemma-3-27B-IT、Llama-3.3-70B-Instruct 和 Qwen3-32B 六种模型。实验设置了标准解码、缓解提示词（mitigation prompting）和确定性解码（T=0）三种条件。结果显示，标准条件下幻觉率在 2.7% 至 99.6% 之间，其中 DrugGPT 表现最佳（2.7-6.4%），而其他模型幻觉率极高（47.2-99.6%）。缓解提示词显著降低了所有模型的幻觉率（p < 0.001），例如 GPT-5-Chat 从 66.4-88.5% 降至 0%。确定性解码对幻觉率无显著影响。研究强调了 LLM 在临床决策中面临的幻觉风险及安全防护的重要性。
why_it_matters: 这篇工作偏基础模型/Agent方向，可能影响病原检测任务的建模上限，值得关注其任务定义与评测设计。
analysis_zh:
## 核心内容
该研究通过一个有趣的“药物还是宝可梦”基准测试，评估了主流大语言模型在临床用药场景下识别虚构信息（幻觉）的能力。研究发现，大多数通用 LLM 在面对混入真实药物列表中的虚构药物（宝可梦名字）时，会错误地为其提供剂量建议和适应症，表现出严重的幻觉问题。

## 为什么相关
在病原检测与临床微生物学领域，AI Agent 常被用于解析临床报告或推荐抗生素方案。如果模型无法区分真实的病原体/药物与虚构或错误的输入，将直接导致错误的临床决策。该研究揭示了基础模型在处理专业实体时的脆弱性，对构建可靠的临床微生物 AI 助手具有警示意义。

## 新颖度判断
**中等偏高**。虽然 LLM 幻觉是已知问题，但该研究通过将“宝可梦”作为负样本引入药物列表，构建了一个直观且具有挑战性的基准任务。此外，研究对比了专门针对药物设计的 DrugGPT 与通用模型（如 GPT-5-Chat、Llama-3.3 等）的差异，并验证了缓解提示词（mitigation prompting）的有效性。

## 方法与证据
- **数据规模**：250 个品牌药列表和 250 个通用药列表，每组含 4-6 种真药和 1 个虚构药。
- **评估模型**：GPT-5-Chat、GPT-4o-mini、DrugGPT、Gemma-3-27B-IT、Llama-3.3-70B-Instruct、Qwen3-32B。
- **实验变量**：标准解码、缓解提示词、确定性解码（T=0）。
- **关键结论**：DrugGPT 幻觉率最低（2.7-6.4%）；通用模型在标准条件下表现极差，幻觉率最高达 99.6%；缓解提示词能显著降低风险（GPT-5-Chat 降至 0%），而单纯降低温度（T=0）无效。

## 应用价值
为临床 AI 应用提供了安全评估框架。研究证明，通过特定的提示词工程（缓解提示词）可以显著增强模型对非法输入的拒绝能力，这对于开发病原基因组分析或耐药性预测的 AI 工具至关重要，有助于减少误报。

## 局限与风险
- **任务单一性**：仅使用了宝可梦名字作为虚构药物，实际临床中可能存在更具迷惑性的拼写错误或罕见药物名，摘要未说明模型在这些情况下的表现。
- **模型版本**：摘要中提到了 GPT-5-Chat，考虑到目前公开技术进展，需警惕该数据是否基于特定预发布版本或模拟环境。
- **泛化性**：缓解提示词在不同领域的泛化效果（如识别虚构的病原体菌株）仍需进一步验证。

title: It's High Time: A Survey of Temporal Question Answering
abstract: Time plays a critical role in how information is generated, retrieved, and interpreted. In this survey, we provide a comprehensive overview of Temporal Question Answering (TQA), a research area that focuses on answering questions involving temporal constraints or context. As time-stamped content from sources like news articles, web archives, and knowledge bases continues to grow, TQA systems must address challenges such as detecting temporal intent, normalizing time expressions, ordering events, and reasoning over evolving or ambiguous facts. We organize existing work through a unified perspective that captures the interaction between corpus temporality, question temporality, and model capabilities, enabling a systematic comparison of datasets, tasks, and approaches. We review recent advances in TQA enabled by neural architectures, especially transformer-based models and Large Language Models (LLMs), highlighting progress in temporal language modeling, retrieval-augmented generation (RAG), and temporal reasoning. We also discuss benchmark datasets and evaluation strategies designed to test temporal robustness,
abstract_zh: 时间在信息的生成、检索和解释中起着至关重要的作用。本综述全面概述了时间问答（TQA）这一研究领域，该领域专注于回答涉及时间约束或背景的问题。随着来自新闻、网络档案和知识库的带时间戳内容不断增长，TQA 系统必须解决检测时间意图、规范化时间表达式、事件排序以及对演变或模糊事实进行推理等挑战。本文通过统一的视角组织现有工作，捕捉语料库时间性、问题时间性与模型能力之间的相互作用，从而实现对数据集、任务和方法的系统比较。我们回顾了由神经架构（特别是 Transformer 模型和大型语言模型 LLM）推动的 TQA 最新进展，重点介绍了时间语言建模、检索增强生成（RAG）和时间推理方面的进步。此外，我们还讨论了旨在测试时间鲁棒性的基准数据集和评估策略。
why_it_matters: 这篇工作偏数据集或基准构建，适合判断是否能作为病原组学训练或评测资源。
analysis_zh:
## 核心内容
该综述系统性地梳理了时间问答（TQA）领域的研究现状。核心内容涵盖了 TQA 系统在处理带时间戳数据时的四大关键挑战：时间意图检测、时间表达式规范化、事件排序以及针对演变或模糊事实的推理。文章提出了一个整合语料库、问题和模型能力的统一框架，并重点评估了 LLM 和 RAG 技术在时间推理中的表现。

## 为什么相关
该研究与病原组学和临床微生物学存在**弱相关性（方法论交叉）**。病原体的演化（如病毒变异时间轴）、临床感染病程的进展以及耐药性的动态演变均具有极强的时间属性。TQA 的技术框架（如对演变事实的推理）可被借鉴用于构建能够解析临床病历时间线或追踪病原进化文献的智能 Agent。

## 新颖度判断
**1.5（较低）**。作为一篇综述类文章，其主要价值在于对现有 TQA 任务和数据集的系统化分类和比较，而非提出全新的算法架构。其“统一视角”为评价模型的时间鲁棒性提供了标准参考，但在 AI 算法层面创新有限。

## 方法与证据
综述采用了“统一视角”（unified perspective）方法，对现有的 TQA 工作进行了分类。证据链基于对 Transformer 架构和 LLM 在时间语言建模、检索增强生成（RAG）等方面的性能回顾。摘要提到该研究对比了现有的基准数据集和评估策略，但摘要内未列出具体的数据集名称（如 TempQuestions 或 TimeQA）或具体的实验指标数值。

## 应用价值
对于病原组学领域，该工作的价值在于提供了一套构建“时间感知型”数据集的逻辑框架。研究者可参考其关于“演变事实推理”的论述，开发针对病原体基因组演变或流行病学趋势的评测基准，从而提升 LLM 在处理具有时效性生物医学信息时的准确性。

## 局限与风险
**弱 AI 交叉风险**：该综述主要基于通用领域（如新闻、Web 档案）的数据，未涉及生物医学或临床微生物学的特定语境。通用 TQA 模型在处理生物大分子进化时间尺度或复杂的临床干预时间线时，可能存在领域迁移失效的风险。摘要未说明模型在处理长程时间跨度数据时的具体表现。

title: SlideAgent: Hierarchical Agentic Framework for Multi-Page Visual Document Understanding
abstract: Retrieval-augmented generation (RAG) extends large language models (LLMs) with external knowledge, but it must balance limited effective context, redundant retrieved evidence, and the loss of fine-grained facts under aggressive compression. Pure compression-based approaches reduce input size but often discard fine-grained details essential for factual accuracy. We propose SARA, a hybrid RAG framework that targets answer quality under fixed token budgets by combining natural-language snippets with semantic compression vectors. SARA retains a small set of passages in text form to preserve entities and numerical values, compresses the remaining evidence into interpretable vectors for broader coverage, and uses those vectors for iterative evidence reranking. Across 9 datasets and 5 open-source LLMs spanning 3 model families (Mistral, Llama, and Gemma), SARA consistently improves answer relevance (+17.71), answer correctness (+13.72), and semantic similarity (+15.53), demonstrating the importance of integrating textual and compressed representations for robust, context-efficient RAG.
abstract_zh: 检索增强生成（RAG）扩展了大型语言模型（LLM）的外部知识，但必须在有限的有效上下文、冗余检索证据以及激进压缩导致的事实丢失之间取得平衡。纯压缩方法虽能减小输入规模，但常丢弃对事实准确性至关重要的细粒度细节。本文提出 SARA，一种混合 RAG 框架，旨在固定 Token 预算下通过结合自然语言片段与语义压缩向量来提升回答质量。SARA 以文本形式保留少量关键段落以保护实体和数值信息，将其余证据压缩为可解释向量以实现更广泛的覆盖，并利用这些向量进行迭代证据重排序。在涵盖 Mistral、Llama 和 Gemma 三个系列、5 个开源 LLM 及 9 个数据集的实验中，SARA 显著提升了回答相关性（+17.71）、正确性（+13.72）和语义相似度（+15.53），证明了整合文本与压缩表示对于构建稳健、高效上下文 RAG 的重要性。
why_it_matters: 这篇工作更像方法创新，可能直接关联 metagenomics、long-read 或 pathogen identification 流程优化。
analysis_zh:
## 核心内容
该研究提出了名为 SARA 的混合检索增强生成（RAG）框架。其核心逻辑是在有限的 Token 预算内，不再简单地对所有检索到的文档进行全文输入或盲目压缩，而是采取“分级处理”策略：保留关键的文本片段（Snippet）以确保实体和数值的精确性，同时将海量背景证据压缩为语义向量以扩大信息覆盖面，并通过迭代重排序优化证据质量。

## 为什么相关
在病原体基因组学和临床微生物学领域，研究人员经常需要从海量文献或长篇临床指南中提取极其精确的信息（如特定的耐药基因位点、病毒株编号或药物剂量）。传统的 RAG 可能会因为上下文窗口限制而丢失关键细节，或因过度压缩导致数值错误。SARA 这种“保留关键文本+压缩背景信息”的思路，直接关联到如何高效处理长程基因组分析报告或多篇病原体研究论文的自动化综述任务。

## 新颖度判断
中等偏上。该工作避开了“全文本”与“全压缩”的二元对立，提出了一种混合表征（Hybrid Representation）方案。其新颖之处在于利用压缩向量进行“迭代证据重排序”，这比单次检索更具动态调整能力，有效解决了长文本处理中的信息损耗问题。

## 方法与证据
研究使用了 9 个数据集进行基准测试，并跨越了 Mistral、Llama 和 Gemma 三个主流开源模型家族（共 5 个 LLM）。实验结果显示，相比基准方法，SARA 在三项关键指标上均有显著提升：回答相关性提升 17.71 点，回答正确性提升 13.72 点，语义相似度提升 15.53 点。这些量化指标有力证明了混合表征在维持事实准确性方面的优势。

## 应用价值
在临床微生物学决策支持系统中，该方法可用于构建更可靠的 AI 助手。例如，在分析多份复杂的宏基因组测序（mNGS）报告时，系统可以保留具体的病原体丰度数值（文本形式），同时将背景流行病学资料压缩处理，从而在不超出模型窗口的前提下，提供既包含精确数据又具备广泛背景参考的诊断建议。

## 局限与风险
摘要未详细说明“迭代证据重排序”带来的额外计算开销和推理延迟。在临床实时诊断场景下，多轮迭代可能影响响应速度。此外，摘要未提及该框架在处理非结构化生物信息学数据（如原始序列字符串）时的表现，其压缩向量是否会丢失生物序列的特异性特征尚不明确。

title: Knowledge Capsules: Structured Nonparametric Memory Units for LLMs
abstract: Large language models (LLMs) encode knowledge in parametric weights, making it costly to update or extend without retraining. Retrieval-augmented generation (RAG) mitigates this limitation by appending retrieved text to the input, but operates purely through context expansion, where external knowledge competes as tokens within the attention mechanism. As a result, its influence is indirect and often unstable, particularly in long context and multi hop reasoning scenarios. We propose Knowledge Capsules, structured nonparametric memory units that represent normalized relational knowledge and can be constructed directly from document corpora using a frozen base model. Instead of injecting knowledge as text, we introduce an External Key Value Injection (KVI) framework that compiles capsules into attention-compatible key value representations, enabling external knowledge to directly participate in the model's attention computation. By shifting knowledge integration from context-level augmentation to memory level interaction, the proposed framework consistently outperforms RAG and GraphRAG across multiple QA benchmarks, with improved stability and accuracy in long context and multi hop reasoning, while requiring no parameter updates.
abstract_zh: 大语言模型（LLMs）将知识存储在参数权重中，导致在不进行重新训练的情况下更新或扩展知识的成本极高。检索增强生成（RAG）虽能缓解此问题，但其通过上下文扩展引入外部知识，使外部信息在注意力机制中作为 Token 与输入竞争，导致其影响是间接且不稳定的，尤其在长文本和多跳推理场景下表现欠缺。本文提出“知识胶囊”（Knowledge Capsules），这是一种结构化的非参数化记忆单元，代表标准化的关系知识，可利用冻结的基础模型直接从文档库构建。我们引入了外部键值注入（KVI）框架，将胶囊编译为与注意力机制兼容的键值（KV）表示，使外部知识直接参与模型的注意力计算。实验表明，该框架在多个问答基准测试中持续优于 RAG 和 GraphRAG，在长文本和多跳推理中提升了稳定性和准确性，且无需更新模型参数。
why_it_matters: 这篇工作更像方法创新，可能直接关联 metagenomics、long-read 或 pathogen identification 流程优化。
analysis_zh:
## 核心内容
该研究提出了一种名为“知识胶囊”的结构化非参数化记忆单元，以及配套的外部键值注入（KVI）框架。其核心逻辑是将外部文档库中的关系知识转化为标准化的 KV（Key-Value）表示，直接注入到 LLM 的注意力机制层中，而非像传统 RAG 那样将其作为文本拼接在 Prompt 中。这种方法实现了知识从“上下文级增强”向“记忆级交互”的转变。

## 为什么相关
在病原微生物学和基因组学领域，研究者经常需要处理海量的结构化知识（如物种分类学、抗生素耐药基因数据库、代谢通路图谱）。传统的 RAG 在处理复杂的生物学多跳推理（例如：从特定序列片段推断其所属质粒，再关联到具体的耐药表型）时，常因 Token 竞争和上下文长度限制导致准确度下降。该方法提供的结构化记忆注入，可能为宏基因组长序列分析和病原体精准鉴定提供更稳定的知识检索支持。

## 新颖度判断
新颖度较高（0.75）。该工作跳出了“文本换文本”的 RAG 范式，通过 KVI 框架实现了外部知识与 Transformer 注意力层的直接解耦与融合。相比于 GraphRAG 仍依赖文本生成，这种直接操作 KV 缓存（KV Cache）层面的技术路径在处理非参数化知识更新上具有显著的架构创新。

## 方法与证据
研究采用了冻结的基础模型（Frozen base model）来构建知识胶囊，并开发了 KVI 框架进行知识编译。实验通过多个问答（QA）基准测试进行验证，结果显示该方法在长文本（Long context）和多跳推理（Multi-hop reasoning）任务中的表现优于 RAG 和 GraphRAG。摘要未说明具体的基准测试名称和具体的百分比提升指标，但强调了其在无需参数更新（No parameter updates）前提下的持续优越性。

## 应用价值
在临床微生物学 AI Agent 的开发中，该技术可用于构建高性能的“病原体知识库”。通过将最新的流行病学数据或耐药性指南转化为“知识胶囊”，模型可以在不频繁微调的情况下，直接在注意力层调用这些专业知识，从而优化长读长（Long-read）测序数据的实时比对与功能注释流程。

## 局限与风险
摘要未明确说明构建“知识胶囊”的计算开销，以及该框架对不同架构 LLM 的通用性。此外，虽然解决了 Token 竞争问题，但大规模知识注入对推理延迟（Latency）的影响尚不清楚。在临床决策等高风险场景下，非参数化记忆的幻觉控制机制仍需进一步验证。

title: From Research Question to Scientific Workflow: Leveraging Agentic AI for Science Automation
abstract: Scientific workflow systems automate execution -- scheduling, fault tolerance, resource management -- but not the semantic translation that precedes it. Scientists still manually convert research questions into workflow specifications, a task requiring both domain knowledge and infrastructure expertise. We propose an agentic architecture that closes this gap through three layers: an LLM interprets natural language into structured intents (semantic layer); validated generators produce reproducible workflow DAGs (deterministic layer); and domain experts author ``Skills'': markdown documents encoding vocabulary mappings, parameter constraints, and optimization strategies (knowledge layer). This decomposition confines LLM non-determinism to intent extraction: identical intents always yield identical workflows. We implement and evaluate the architecture on the 1000 Genomes population genetics workflow and Hyperflow WMS running on Kubernetes. In an ablation study on 150 queries, Skills raise full-match intent accuracy from 44% to 83%; skill-driven deferred workflow generation reduces data transfer by 92\%; and the end-to-end pipeline completes queries on Kubernetes with LLM overhead below 15 seconds and cost under $0.001 per query.
abstract_zh: 科学工作流系统虽能实现调度、容错和资源管理等自动化执行，但仍缺乏前期的语义转换能力。科学家目前必须手动将研究问题转化为工作流规范，这要求同时具备领域知识和基础设施专业经验。本文提出一种三层智能体架构来填补这一空白：语义层利用大语言模型（LLM）将自然语言解析为结构化意图；确定性层通过验证生成器产生可复现的工作流有向无环图（DAG）；知识层则由领域专家编写“技能（Skills）”文档，定义词汇映射、参数约束和优化策略。该架构将 LLM 的不确定性严格限制在语义提取环节，确保相同意图始终生成一致的工作流。研究在 1000 基因组计划群体遗传学工作流及 Kubernetes 上的 Hyperflow 系统中进行了验证。消减实验表明，“技能”层将意图匹配准确率从 44% 提升至 83%，使数据传输量减少 92%，且单次查询的 LLM 开销低于 15 秒，成本不足 0.001 美元。
why_it_matters: 这篇工作偏基础模型/Agent方向，可能影响病原检测任务的建模上限，值得关注其任务定义与评测设计。
analysis_zh:
## 核心内容
该研究开发了一种基于智能体（Agent）的科学自动化架构，旨在将自然语言形式的研究问题自动转化为可执行的科学工作流。核心设计包括三层结构：LLM 负责意图提取（语义层）、验证生成器负责 DAG 构建（确定性层）、专家文档提供约束（知识层/Skills）。其核心逻辑是通过解耦语义理解与逻辑生成，解决 LLM 在复杂科学计算中的不可预测性问题。

## 为什么相关
在病原基因组学和临床微生物学领域，分析流程（如物种鉴定、耐药基因筛查、进化树构建）通常涉及复杂的生物信息学工具链。该研究提出的架构能够降低生物学家使用高性能计算资源的门槛，通过自然语言驱动复杂的生物信息学分析流（如摘要中提到的 1000 基因组计划工作流），实现从“科学设想”到“代码执行”的自动化衔接。

## 新颖度判断
新颖度中等（0.5）。其创新点不在于提出了新的 LLM 模型，而在于提出了一种“约束型 Agent 架构”。通过引入专家编写的 Markdown “技能”文档作为知识层，成功将 LLM 的随机性限制在语义解析阶段，解决了科学实验中至关重要的“可复现性”痛点，这比单纯的 Prompt Engineering 更具工程参考价值。

## 方法与证据
研究采用了消减实验（Ablation Study）进行验证，实验对象为 1000 基因组计划（1000 Genomes）群体遗传学工作流，运行环境为 Kubernetes 上的 Hyperflow WMS。证据显示：在引入“技能”层后，意图匹配准确率从 44% 大幅提升至 83%；通过技能驱动的延迟生成策略，数据传输量显著减少 92%；性能指标上，LLM 额外开销控制在 15 秒内，成本极低（<$0.001/query）。

## 应用价值
对于临床微生物实验室，该技术可用于构建“对话式生信分析平台”。临床医生或研究员只需输入“分析该样本中副溶血性弧菌的毒力基因分布”，系统即可依据预设的专家技能文档，自动调用相应的生物信息学工具和计算资源，生成标准化的分析报告，极大地提高了科研效率并减少了基础设施操作门槛。

## 局限与风险
摘要未说明该架构在处理极其复杂、跨学科或未定义“技能”的新型研究问题时的表现。此外，虽然 LLM 成本低，但系统对专家编写“技能”文档的依赖程度较高，如果领域专家定义的参数约束或词汇映射存在偏差，可能会导致生成的工作流虽然逻辑一致但科学性错误。摘要亦未提及对多模态数据（如电泳图、质谱图）的直接处理能力。

title: Generalizing intensive care AI across time scales in resource-limited settings
abstract: Temporal resolution of physiological monitoring in intensive care varies widely across healthcare systems. Artificial intelligence models assume a uniform and fixed frequency of sampling, thus limiting the generalizability of models, especially to resource-limited settings. Here, we propose a novel resolution-transfer task for physiological time series and ask whether models trained on high-resolution data can generalize to a low data-density setting without the need to retrain them. SafeICU, a novel longitudinal pediatric intensive care dataset spanning ten years from a tertiary care hospital in India, was used to test this hypothesis. Self-supervised transformer models were trained on 144,271 patient-hours of high-resolution physiological signals from 984 pediatric ICU stays to learn representations of heart rate, respiratory rate, oxygen saturation, and arterial blood pressure. Transfer of this model to low-resolution data established robust performance in clinically relevant lower-frequency intervals, consistently outperforming models trained directly at coarser resolutions. Further, these representations generalized across patient populations, maintaining performance when evaluated on adult intensive care cohorts from the MIMIC-III and eICU databases without retraining. In a downstream task of early shock prediction, models achieved strong discrimination in the pediatric cohort (area under the receiver operating characteristic curve (AUROC) 0.87; area under the precision-recall curve (AUPRC) 0.92) and retained stable performance across monitoring intervals from 10 to 60 minutes (AUROC 0.78-0.88). Together, these results demonstrate that physiological representations learned from high-resolution data enable time-scale-robust and transferable AI for intensive care. The publicly released SafeICU dataset, comprising longitudinal vital signs, laboratory measurements, treatment records, microbiology, and admission and discharge, provides a foundation for developing and deploying generalizable clinical AI in resource-limited settings.
abstract_zh: 重症监护中的生理监测时间分辨率在不同医疗系统中差异巨大，而现有AI模型通常假设采样频率固定，限制了其在资源受限地区的推广。本研究提出了一种针对生理时间序列的新型分辨率迁移任务，探讨在高分辨率数据上训练的模型能否在不重新训练的情况下推广到低数据密度环境。研究利用印度一家三级医院长达十年的新型儿科重症监护数据集SafeICU，包含984次住院的144,271个患者小时的高分辨率生理信号。研究采用自监督Transformer模型学习心率、呼吸频率、血氧饱和度和动脉血压的表征。结果显示，该模型在临床相关的低频区间表现稳健，优于直接在粗糙分辨率下训练的模型。此外，该表征可跨人群推广，在无需重新训练的情况下，于MIMIC-III和eICU成人队列中保持了性能。在早期休克预测任务中，模型在儿科队列中达到0.87的AUROC，且在10至60分钟的监测间隔内表现稳定。SafeICU数据集包含生命体征、微生物学等记录，为资源受限地区的临床AI部署奠定了基础。
why_it_matters: 这篇工作更接近临床/监测落地，适合评估其对快速识别、预警或治疗辅助的实际价值。
analysis_zh:
## 核心内容
该研究针对重症监护（ICU）中生理监测频率不一致的问题，提出了一种“分辨率迁移”任务。通过在印度SafeICU数据集（144,271患者小时）的高分辨率生理信号上训练自监督Transformer模型，证明了高频数据训练的模型可以有效泛化至低频采样环境，解决了AI模型在资源受限医疗机构中因数据稀疏而难以部署的痛点。

## 为什么相关
本研究属于临床微生物学与临床监测的交叉领域。摘要明确提到SafeICU数据集包含“微生物学（microbiology）”记录、实验室测量和治疗记录。在临床实践中，败血症或感染性休克的早期识别（如摘要中的休克预测任务）高度依赖于生理体征与微生物检测结果的整合，该模型为在资源有限环境下结合多模态数据进行感染监测提供了技术路径。

## 新颖度判断
中等偏高。其新颖性在于提出了“分辨率迁移”这一特定任务，打破了传统AI模型对固定采样频率的依赖。此外，研究利用了来自非西方国家（印度）的大规模儿科ICU数据集，并成功在MIMIC-III和eICU等成人数据集上实现了跨人群的零样本（without retraining）泛化，这在临床时间序列AI中较为少见。

## 方法与证据
1. **模型与任务**：使用自监督Transformer模型处理心率、呼吸率、血氧和血压信号。
2. **数据规模**：基于SafeICU数据集，涵盖984次儿科ICU住院记录，总计144,271患者小时。
3. **关键指标**：在早期休克预测中，儿科队列AUROC达0.87，AUPRC达0.92。在监测间隔从10分钟延长至60分钟时，AUROC仍保持在0.78-0.88之间，证明了对时间尺度的鲁棒性。
4. **泛化验证**：模型未经重新训练直接应用于MIMIC-III和eICU成人数据库，表现依然稳定。

## 应用价值
该研究具有极强的临床落地潜力，特别是在电力或设备受限、无法进行高频电子监测的基层医院。通过发布包含微生物学和生命体征的SafeICU数据集，为开发针对败血症、休克等重症的通用型预警工具提供了基础。其“高频训练、低频应用”的模式降低了AI在欠发达地区的使用门槛。

## 局限与风险
摘要未详细说明模型在处理极端缺失值或非周期性采样数据时的表现。虽然在休克预测中表现良好，但AUROC在监测间隔拉长时从0.88降至0.78，显示出性能仍受采样频率下降的影响。此外，SafeICU虽为十年纵向数据，但源自单一中心，其微生物学特征与生理体征的关联机制在摘要中未充分展开。

title: Tuberculosis in households with infectious cases in Kampala city: Harnessing health data science for new insights on an ancient disease with persistent, unresolved problems (DS-IAFRICA TB) study protocol
abstract: Tuberculosis (TB) is prevalent in Uganda and overlaps with a high rate of HIV/TB coinfection. While nearly all hospital-based TB cases in Kampala, the capital of Uganda, show clear TB symptoms, 30% or more of undiagnosed TB cases found through active screening are asymptomatic. Additionally, the host risk factors for TB in Kampala cannot be distinguished from environmental risk factors. These TB-specific challenges are just part of the complexity, especially in areas with high HIV/AIDS burden. Data science techniques, especially Artificial Intelligence (AI) and Machine Learning (ML) algorithms, could help untangle this complexity by identifying factors related to the host, pathogen, and environment, which are difficult to explain or predict with traditional/conventional methods. In this project, we will use health data science approaches (AI/ML) to identify factors driving TB transmission within households and reasons for anti-TB treatment failure. We will utilize the computational resources at Makerere University and available demographic, clinical, and laboratory data from TB patients and their contacts to develop AI and ML algorithms. These will aim to: (1) identify patients at baseline (month 0) unlikely to convert their sputum or culture results by months 2 and 5, thus at risk of failing TB treatment; (2) identify household contacts of TB cases who are at risk of developing TB disease, as well as contacts who may resist TB infection despite repeated exposure to M. tuberculosis. Achieving these objectives will provide evidence that data science methods are effective for early detection of potential TB cases and high-risk patients, thereby helping to reduce TB transmission in the community. The study protocol received approval from the School of Biomedical Sciences IRB, protocol number SBS-2023-495.
abstract_zh: 乌干达坎帕拉市结核病（TB）流行严重，且伴随高比例的HIV/TB共感染。尽管医院确诊病例多有明显症状，但主动筛查发现的未诊断病例中，30%以上为无症状。由于宿主与环境风险因素交织，传统方法难以解释复杂的传播机制。本研究（DS-IAFRICA TB）旨在利用健康数据科学（AI/ML）技术，分析驱动家庭内部传播及导致抗结核治疗失败的因素。依托麦克雷雷大学的计算资源，研究将整合TB患者及其接触者的人口统计学、临床和实验室数据。核心目标包括：(1) 在基线期（第0月）识别出第2和第5个月痰液或培养结果无法转阴的治疗失败高风险患者；(2) 识别易患结核病的家庭接触者，以及在反复暴露于结核分枝杆菌后仍能抵抗感染的接触者。该方案旨在证明数据科学在早期发现潜在病例和高危患者、减少社区传播方面的有效性。
why_it_matters: 这篇工作更接近临床/监测落地，适合评估其对快速识别、预警或治疗辅助的实际价值。
analysis_zh:
## 核心内容
该研究是一项临床研究方案（Protocol），旨在利用人工智能与机器学习（AI/ML）技术解决乌干达坎帕拉市结核病（TB）防控中的复杂问题。研究重点关注两个维度：一是预测患者的治疗预后（如痰液转阴情况），二是识别家庭接触者中的易感人群与具有免疫抵抗力的个体（Resisters）。

## 为什么相关
本研究属于临床微生物学与AI的交叉应用。它试图通过数据科学手段解决临床痛点：如何从复杂的宿主、病原体和环境数据中提取特征，以预测TB的治疗失败和家庭传播风险。对于病原生信领域，研究中提到的“抵抗感染的接触者”可能涉及宿主遗传学或免疫组学数据的挖掘。

## 新颖度判断
新颖度中等偏上（5.25）。其创新点在于：1. 针对高HIV负担地区的复杂背景进行建模；2. 明确提出利用AI识别“暴露后未感染”的特殊人群（Resisters），这在传统流行病学中较难精准定义；3. 强调在基线期（0月）即对2个月和5个月后的治疗结果进行早期预测。

## 方法与证据
摘要提到将使用麦克雷雷大学的计算资源开发AI/ML算法。数据来源包括：人口统计学、临床表现和实验室检测数据。评价指标明确为：基线期预测第2个月和第5个月的痰液/培养转阴情况。由于是研究方案，摘要未说明具体的模型架构（如随机森林、神经网络等）或具体的特征权重，也未提供初步实验结果。

## 应用价值
该研究具有较强的临床落地潜力。若模型开发成功，可实现：1. 临床分诊优化，对预测治疗失败的患者加强监测或调整方案；2. 精准预防，识别高风险家庭接触者进行干预；3. 科学研究价值，通过AI识别出的“抵抗者”特征可能为新型疫苗或药物研发提供靶点。

## 局限与风险
1. **研究阶段限制**：目前仅为研究方案（Protocol），缺乏实际模型表现数据（如AUC、准确率等）。
2. **数据局限性**：摘要未说明是否包含病原体基因组数据或宿主多组学数据，若仅依赖临床和人口学数据，模型预测上限可能受限。
3. **泛化性风险**：在坎帕拉市开发的人工智能模型是否适用于其他结核病高流行地区（如亚洲或非洲其他城市）尚不明确。

title: AI-readiness criteria for biomedical data
abstract: Biomedical research is rapidly adopting artificial intelligence (AI). Yet the inherent complexity of biomedical data preparation requires implementing actionable, robust criteria for ethical and explainable AI (XAI) at the "pre-model" stage, encompassing data acquisition, detailed transformations, and ethical governance. Simple conformance to FAIR (Findable, Accessible, Interoperable, Reusable) Principles is insufficient. Here, we define criteria and practices for reliable AI-readiness of biomedical data, developed by the NIH Bridge to Artificial Intelligence (Bridge2AI) Standards Working Group across seven core dimensions of dataset AI-readiness: FAIRness, Provenance, Characterization, Ethics, Pre-model Explainability, Sustainability, and Computability. Conformance to these criteria provides a basis for pre-model scientific rigor and ethical integrity, mitigating downstream risks of bias and error before AI modeling. We apply and evaluate these standards across all four Bridge2AI flagship datasets, spanning functional genomics to clinical medicine, and encode them in machine-actionable metadata bound to the datasets. This framework sets a benchmark for preparing ethical, reusable datasets in biomedical AI and provides standardized methods for reliable pre-model data evaluation.
abstract_zh: 生物医学研究正迅速转向人工智能（AI）驱动模式，但生物医学数据的复杂性要求在“模型前”阶段（涵盖数据获取、转换和伦理治理）实施稳健的可操作标准，以确保AI的伦理性和可解释性（XAI）。研究指出，单纯遵循FAIR原则（可发现、可访问、可互操作、可重用）已不足以满足AI建模需求。为此，NIH Bridge2AI标准工作组定义了生物医学数据“AI就绪”（AI-readiness）的标准与实践，涵盖七个核心维度：FAIR性、溯源性、特征化、伦理、模型前可解释性、可持续性和计算性。遵循这些标准可确保模型前的科学严谨性，在建模前降低下游偏见和错误风险。该框架已在涵盖功能基因组学到临床医学的四个Bridge2AI旗舰数据集上完成应用与评估，并将其编码为与数据集绑定的机器可操作元数据，为构建伦理、可重用的生物医学AI数据集设定了基准。
why_it_matters: 这篇工作偏数据集或基准构建，适合判断是否能作为病原组学训练或评测资源。
analysis_zh:
## 核心内容
该研究由NIH Bridge2AI标准工作组主导，旨在解决生物医学数据在AI应用中的质量瓶颈。核心内容是定义了“AI就绪”（AI-readiness）的七大维度标准：FAIR性、溯源性（Provenance）、特征化（Characterization）、伦理（Ethics）、模型前可解释性（Pre-model Explainability）、可持续性（Sustainability）和计算性（Computability）。其核心逻辑是将质量控制前移至“模型前”阶段，通过机器可操作的元数据来规范数据准备流程。

## 为什么相关
在病原组学、蛋白质组学及临床微生物学领域，数据的高度异质性和伦理敏感性是AI模型落地的主要障碍。该研究提到的“功能基因组学”和“临床医学”数据集直接关联到本领域的核心数据类型。对于构建病原体识别模型或临床耐药预测模型的科研人员而言，该标准提供了一套评估原始数据是否“合格”用于AI训练的量化准则，有助于提升模型的可迁移性和临床可信度。

## 新颖度判断
新颖度较高。该工作突破了传统的FAIR数据原则，首次系统性地提出了针对AI建模全生命周期的“AI-readiness”多维标准。其新颖之处在于提出了“模型前可解释性”和“计算性”等具体维度，并将抽象的伦理准则转化为可操作、可编码的元数据，填补了生物医学数据准备与AI建模之间的工程化空白。

## 方法与证据
研究方法包括理论框架构建与实证评估。证据方面，研究者将该标准应用于四个Bridge2AI旗舰数据集，涵盖了从微观的功能基因组学到宏观的临床医学领域。通过将这些标准编码为“机器可操作元数据”（machine-actionable metadata）并与数据集绑定，验证了框架在不同生物医学场景下的普适性和技术可行性。摘要未说明具体的评估指标数值（如准确率或一致性得分）。

## 应用价值
该框架具有极高的行业标准参考价值。对于病原生物学数据库构建者，可利用该标准优化数据发布流程，使其更易于被大模型（LLM）或下游AI Agent调用。对于临床研究者，该标准中的“伦理”和“溯源性”维度有助于在多中心研究中降低数据偏见，确保AI预测结果在临床微生物诊断中的合规性。

## 局限与风险
摘要中未明确说明这七个维度的具体量化评分细则，可能存在实施过程中的主观判定风险。此外，对于资源受限的实验室，严格遵循这七大维度可能会显著增加数据整理的成本和技术门槛。摘要亦未提及该标准在处理实时动态更新的临床微生物数据（如爆发监测数据）时的表现。

title: GeneBench: Assessing AI Agents for Multi-Stage Inference Problems in Genomics and Quantitative Biology
abstract: We introduce GeneBench, a benchmark for AI agents on realistic multi-stage scientific data analysis in genetics and quantitative biology. Existing biology benchmarks mostly measure knowledge retrieval, execution of routine pipelines, or a single analysis step. Yet they do not capture the broader scope of work that occupies much of computational scientists' time: cleaning and normalizing assay, phenotype, or clinical data; exploratory data analysis; statistical model selection and diagnostic iteration; and producing a conclusion that informs a downstream scientific or translational decision. GeneBench addresses this gap with 103 evaluations targeting quantities of direct practical relevance across 10 domains, with a genomics-centered core and adjacent coverage in other 'omics and quantitative biology settings. Each problem comprises an encapsulated multi-step analysis with staged data, prompts that define a quantity of interest while otherwise providing minimal guidance, and verifiable answers. Solving each problem requires identifying and addressing realistic obstacles such as measurement error, selection bias, confounding, QC failures, and choosing among competing model classes. Through extensive ablation studies, we verify that each problem admits a single defensible answer. Each problem involves multiple dependent decision points; i.e., substantive inferential forks where a plausible wrong choice changes the downstream analysis, such that errors propagate through the inferential chain and into the final graded target. In initial evaluations, the mainline GPT family reaches an eval-level pass rate of 25.0% with GPT-5.5 at the xhigh reasoning setting. In separately reported GPT Pro runs, GPT-5.5 Pro reaches 33.2%, GPT-5.4 Pro reaches 25.6%, and GPT-5.2 Pro reaches 10.8%. Even for the two strongest reported Pro-harness settings, 60.2% and 62.1% of problems, respectively, remain below 20% pass rate over repeated runs. The strongest external baseline, Gemini 3.1 Pro, achieves 11.2%. Models often complete substantial portions of the workflow but exhibit a consistent gap between noticing and acting: they identify local diagnostic signals but fail to propagate the implication to the corresponding analysis decision, and as a result select wrong estimators or persist on initially plausible but incorrect analysis paths. GeneBench therefore measures an emerging capability that remains as yet unreliable.
abstract_zh: 本文介绍了 GeneBench，这是一个针对遗传学和定量生物学中真实多阶段科学数据分析的 AI Agent 基准测试。现有基准多侧重于知识检索或单一分析步骤，而 GeneBench 填补了计算科学家在数据清洗、归一化、探索性数据分析、统计模型选择及诊断迭代等复杂工作流程中的评估空白。该基准包含 10 个领域的 103 项评估任务，以基因组学为核心，涵盖其他组学和定量生物学场景。每个问题均为封装的多步分析，提供分阶段数据和最简引导提示，并设有可验证的答案。解决这些问题需要识别并处理测量误差、选择偏倚、混杂因素和质控（QC）失败等现实障碍。评估结果显示，GPT-5.5 在高推理设置下的通过率为 25.0%，GPT-5.5 Pro 为 33.2%，而 Gemini 3.1 Pro 仅为 11.2%。研究发现，模型虽能识别局部诊断信号，但往往无法将其转化为正确的分析决策，导致错误在推理链中累积。GeneBench 衡量了一种目前尚不可靠的新兴能力。
why_it_matters: 这篇工作偏数据集或基准构建，适合判断是否能作为病原组学训练或评测资源。
analysis_zh:
## 核心内容
该研究构建了名为 GeneBench 的基准测试集，专门用于评估 AI Agent 在遗传学和定量生物学领域处理复杂、多阶段推理问题的能力。该基准包含 103 个评估任务，覆盖 10 个生物学领域，重点考察模型在数据清洗、统计建模、诊断迭代及得出科学结论方面的表现，而非简单的知识问答。

## 为什么相关
在病原组学和临床微生物学研究中，数据分析往往涉及复杂的质控（QC）和多步推断。GeneBench 模拟了真实的计算生物学工作流，其涵盖的“测量误差”、“混杂因素”和“质控失败”等挑战，正是病原体基因组分析和临床多组学数据整合中常见的痛点。该基准可作为评估病原组学自动化分析 Agent 可靠性的核心资源。

## 新颖度判断
高。相比于现有的侧重知识检索（Knowledge Retrieval）或单一流水线执行的基准，GeneBench 首次系统性地提出了“推理分叉点（Inferential Forks）”概念，要求 AI 在没有详细指令的情况下，根据中间结果自主修正分析路径，这更接近真实的科研场景。

## 方法与证据
- **数据规模**：包含 103 个评估任务，跨越 10 个生物学子领域。
- **实验对象**：测试了 GPT-5.5、GPT-5.4、GPT-5.2 及其 Pro 版本，以及 Gemini 3.1 Pro。
- **关键指标**：GPT-5.5 Pro 达到最高通过率 33.2%，而 Gemini 3.1 Pro 仅为 11.2%。
- **核心发现**：模型存在“观察与行动脱节”现象，即能识别出局部异常信号（如 QC 失败），但无法据此调整后续分析策略，导致 60% 以上的问题在多次运行中通过率低于 20%。

## 应用价值
1. **模型评测**：为开发生物医学专用大模型提供了一个高难度的性能度量衡。
2. **Agent 开发**：揭示了当前 Agent 在处理“推理链错误传播”方面的弱点，指导未来在病原组学自动化工具中引入更强的诊断反馈机制。
3. **教学与实战**：任务设计中包含的“现实障碍”（如选择偏倚）可作为生物信息学自动化流程的典型案例库。

## 局限与风险
- **模型性能瓶颈**：摘要显示即使是最强的 GPT-5.5 Pro，通过率也仅为 33.2%，说明 AI Agent 在处理此类任务时仍极不可靠。
- **领域覆盖**：虽然以基因组学为核心，但对于临床微生物学中特有的病原鉴定、耐药基因挖掘等特定任务的覆盖程度，摘要未详细说明。
- **错误累积**：由于任务具有依赖性，初始阶段的微小决策错误会导致最终结果完全偏离，这在临床决策支持中具有较高风险。

title: RELOOP: Recursive Retrieval with Multi-Hop Reasoner and Planners for Heterogeneous QA
abstract: Retrieval-augmented generation (RAG) remains brittle on multi-step questions and heterogeneous evidence sources, trading accuracy against latency and token/tool budgets. This paper introduces RELOOP, a structure aware framework using Hierarchical Sequence (HSEQ) that (i) linearize documents, tables, and knowledge graphs into a reversible hierarchical sequence with lightweight structural tags, and (ii) perform structure-aware iteration to collect just-enough evidence before answer synthesis. A Head Agent provides guidance that leads retrieval, while an Iteration Agent selects and expands HSeq via structure-respecting actions (e.g., parent/child hops, table row/column neighbors, KG relations); Finally the head agent composes canonicalized evidence to genearte the final answer, with an optional refinement loop to resolve detected contradictions. Experiments on HotpotQA (text), HybridQA/TAT-QA (table+text), and MetaQA (KG) show consistent EM/F1 gains over strong single-pass, multi-hop, and agentic RAG baselines with high efficiency. Besides, RELOOP exhibits three key advantages: (1) a format-agnostic unification that enables a single policy to operate across text, tables, and KGs without per-dataset specialization; (2) \textbf{guided, budget-aware iteration} that reduces unnecessary hops, tool calls, and tokens while preserving accuracy; and (3) evidence canonicalization for reliable QA, improving answers consistency and auditability.
abstract_zh: 检索增强生成（RAG）在处理多步复杂问题和异构证据源时仍面临挑战，往往需要在准确性、延迟与计算预算之间进行权衡。本文提出 RELOOP，一种基于分层序列（HSEQ）的结构感知框架。该框架首先将文档、表格和知识图谱（KG）线性化为带有轻量级结构标签的可逆分层序列；随后通过结构感知迭代，在答案合成前收集充足证据。系统中，Head Agent 提供检索引导，Iteration Agent 则通过遵循结构的动作（如父/子节点跳跃、表格行列邻居、KG 关系）选择并扩展 HSeq。最后，Head Agent 整合规范化证据生成最终答案，并利用可选的细化循环解决检测到的矛盾。在 HotpotQA（文本）、HybridQA/TAT-QA（表文混合）和 MetaQA（知识图谱）上的实验表明，RELOOP 在 EM/F1 指标上显著优于单次检索和多跳 RAG 基线。该框架具有格式无关的统一性、预算感知的迭代效率以及证据规范化带来的可审计性。
why_it_matters: 这篇工作偏基础模型/Agent方向，可能影响病原检测任务的建模上限，值得关注其任务定义与评测设计。
analysis_zh:
## 核心内容
该研究提出了 RELOOP 框架，旨在解决 RAG 系统在处理异构数据源（文本、表格、知识图谱）时的多步推理难题。其核心是 HSEQ（分层序列）表示法和双智能体（Head Agent 与 Iteration Agent）协同机制，通过结构感知的递归检索实现高效的证据收集与答案合成。

## 为什么相关
本研究属于“弱 AI 交叉”，其核心贡献在于通用 RAG 架构。但在临床微生物学和病原基因组学领域，研究者经常面临异构数据整合的挑战，例如需要同时检索临床生化指标（表格）、病原体分类学知识图谱（KG）以及最新的耐药性研究文献（文本）。RELOOP 提供的统一处理框架为构建此类复杂的临床决策支持系统提供了技术底座。

## 新颖度判断
新颖度较高（0.75）。其创新点在于：1. 提出 HSEQ 将不同结构的证据统一线性化，避免了为每种数据类型开发专门的检索器；2. 引入了结构感知的迭代动作（如表格行列跳转），使模型能像人类查阅资料一样在异构数据间进行逻辑跳转。

## 方法与证据
- **方法**：采用 HSEQ 线性化技术，并设计了 Head Agent（负责全局引导和证据规范化）与 Iteration Agent（负责执行具体的结构化跳转动作）。
- **实验对象与数据**：在 HotpotQA（多跳文本）、HybridQA/TAT-QA（表格+文本混合）和 MetaQA（知识图谱）等多个基准数据集上进行了验证。
- **指标结论**：实验显示 RELOOP 在 EM（精确匹配）和 F1 分数上均优于强基线模型，且通过“预算感知迭代”减少了不必要的工具调用和 Token 消耗。

## 应用价值
在病原学研究中，该方法可用于构建“异构知识问答 Agent”。例如，当研究员询问“某种特定突变在特定地区的流行趋势及临床表现”时，RELOOP 可以自动在基因组元数据表、地理分布知识图谱和流行病学报告中递归检索并整合证据，提高答案的准确性和可追溯性（可审计性）。

## 局限与风险
摘要未说明该模型在特定垂直领域（如医学或生物信息学）专业术语上的对齐效果。此外，虽然提到了“细化循环”来解决矛盾，但在处理高度专业且可能存在冲突的临床证据时，该机制的鲁棒性尚未在生物医学场景下得到验证。实验仅限于通用数据集，缺乏对大规模生物数据库检索延迟的评估。
